preamble: The transformation of the close-mid front unrounded vowel /e/ to the nasalized close front unrounded vowel /ĩ/ is a documented phonetic shift. This change involves two primary processes: raising of the vowel from close-mid to close, and the acquisition of nasalization, often triggered by the influence of an adjacent nasal consonant.
phoneticEffects: Vowel raising, nasalization, frontness maintained, unroundedness maintained

examples.note: Nasalization and raising of /e/ to /ĩ/ can occur in environments influenced by nasal consonants.
Nasalization can lead to height adjustments in vowels, including the raising of /e/ to /ĩ/.
Similar to other languages, Igbo exhibits phonetic effects of nasalization on vowel height, potentially including this shift.

sources: Beddor, P. S. (1983). Phonological and Phonetic Effects of Nasalization on Vowel Height. Indiana University Linguistics Club.
